paper_title: Theoretical and experimental assessment of the effect of adhesive bond thickness on the flexural capacity of CFRP strengthened beams

entities.description: Author of the research paper from the University of Benin.
Author of the research paper from the University of Benin.
Department of Civil Engineering, University of Benin, Nigeria.
The primary field of research for the study.

entities.id: Structural strengthening of reinforced concrete (RC) elements
entities.description: The process of improving the load-carrying capacity of concrete structures using external materials.

entities.id: Flexural capacity of CFRP strengthened beams
entities.description: The research problem investigating how adhesive thickness affects the bending strength of beams.
The thickness of the material used to bond CFRP to concrete surfaces.
Guide for the Design and Construction of Externally Bonded FRP Systems for Strengthening Concrete Structures.
Material used for structural strengthening, specifically wraps HM-30, HM-45, and HM-60.
Chemical agents used to bond CFRP fabrics to RC beams.
Device used to measure the thickness of the adhesive layers.
Theoretical procedure used to obtain the moment of resistance of unstrengthened beams.

entities.id: Experimental test data of 38 RC beams
entities.description: Numerical records and measurements obtained from laboratory tests on 38 cast beams.
The physical length of the reinforced concrete beams used in the experiment.
The mechanical property value for all CFRP wraps used.
The flexural capacity value obtained experimentally for the control specimen.
The capacity value obtained from theoretical calculations for the control specimen.
The estimated shear strength of the original beam.
The shear force obtained for an HM-60 strengthened beam.
